add sgl_per_tensor_quant_fp8 to replace vllm ops.scaled_fp8_quant, remove a dependency of a vllm kernel.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Core] fix abort_seq_group and memory leak when n>1
vllm.author: courage17340
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is to fix two bugs when there are requests with n>1:

Requests with n>1 can't be aborted correctly. The log says they are aborted, but they are still running in fact.
Elements in self.seq_id_to_seq_group are never deleted.

It's easy to reproduce the first bug: serve a model, make a curl req

vllm.title: [Core] Don't use cache during multi-modal profiling
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Avoid using cache during profiling step so that the dummy outputs (which are intentionally constructed to take up as much memory as possible) can be discarded immediately afterwards.

vllm.title: [RLHF] use worker_extension_cls for compatibility with V0 and V1
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
RLHF users, right now, inherit the worker class to extend available collective_rpc call. As we migrate to V1, the worker class they need to inherit will change.
This PR adds a worker_adapter_cls argument, which will be inheritted by vLLM's worker class automatically. Then RLHF users don't need to ca

vllm.title: Reinstate `best_of` for V0
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Due to a misunderstanding on my part, best_of got erroneously removed from V0 in #13997.
This PR reinstates:

best_of in the protocols and SamplingParams with a note that it is only supported in V0
The error if best_of is used (and is not 1) with V1 and the test that makes sure this happens

This PR provides CPU support for the s390x architecture. Additional PRs in future will optimize the performance and will enable build and test through buildkite.
To build the image for the s390x architecture:
docker build --format docker -t vllm:v0.7.0-cpu -f Dockerfile.s390x .

I noticed that V1 Test is failing on main branch ever since we merged: #14243
The error suggests that the monkeypatch that is used in test_engine_core_client does not get picked up when using spawn. It looks like this is a known issue with monkeypatch.
If we really need to use spawn for this test, a

vllm.title: [Kernel] Add needs_fixed_stride_order tag to most GEMMs
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt: PyTorch 2.6.0 changed the default behavior for custom operators to be "requires_contiguous". In GEMM kernels, we often transpose or otherwise transform our weights offline, which would be in conflict with this change. This PR changes adds {at::Tag::needs_fixed_stride_order} to most GEMMs in csrc/tor

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix use_direct_call condition in FusedMoE layer for
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #14305 made it conditional whether or not to do some of the setup code related to wrapping the FusedMoE layer in a custom op. But I got the condition backwards 🤦.

vllm.title: [Bug] Fix Attention when ignored in by quant_method
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: There was a bug in the Attention module when a quantized model has the attention module ignored. Many quant_config.get_quant_method calls will return UnquantizedLinearMethod rather than None if the layer is ignored. Maybe we should be returning UnquantizedAttentionMethod to be more clear, but that i

vllm.title: [V1][Bugfix] Standardize quantized kv cache rejection for attention backends
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Many users have been reporting FP8 KV cache "hasn't been working right" in V1, but in reality we have just been ignoring the parameter since the only (non-MLA) attention backend in V1 is FlashAttention, which doesn't support it. This PR audits existing backends to raise an exception quickly if quant
